DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF MAUDELLE MITCHELL'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IRISARRY ARMANDO VASQUES AND REQUEST FOR REASONABLE SANCTIONS, GIVEN THE CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE, THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED UNTIL FEBRUARY 16, 2016 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, DISCOVERY, TO GIVE DEFENSE COUNSEL ONE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO LOCATE DEFENDANT VASQUES AND OBTAIN FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO THE OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY. IF THE RESPONSES HAVE NOT BEEN SERVED BY THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE, THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING/MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE GRANTED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Deft. Irisarry Armando Vasques And Request For Reasonable Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Continued To Feb-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF MAUDELLE MITCHELL's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendant Irisarry Armando Vasques And Request For Reasonable Sanctions, given the continuance of the trial date, the hearing on this motion is continued until February 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302, Discovery, to give Defense counsel one final opportunity to locate Defendant Vasques and obtain full and complete responses to the outstanding discovery. If the responses have not been served by the continued hearing date, the motion for terminating/monetary sanctions will be granted. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Appearances:
Eran Lagstein, Esq. of Lagstein Law Firm, 323.330.0507, appearing for the Plaintiff Mitchell.
David M. Hillings, Esq. of Prindle Amaro Goetz Hillyard Barnes et al, appearing for the Defendants, Speck Cab, et al.

The parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem David McDonald. Matter A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as modified: PLAINTIFF MAUDELLE MITCHELL'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories To Defendant Robiel Gebretatiyos Set One And Request For Reasonable Sanctions is granted. Responses to be served no later than October 21, 2015. The Court notes that Defendants have filed a motion for protective order on this same discovery to be heard on October 27, 2015. While that motion is not presently at issue, the Court has reviewed Defendants' arguments and they do not change this tentative ruling. Sanctions of $300 awarded to Plaintiff. Counsel to prepare the order. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter not reported. (302/JPT)
